title: Introducing the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
snippet: MCP is an open protocol that standardises how applications provide context, tools and prompts to LLMs, decoupling model clients from integrations.

title: MCP transports: stdio and streamable HTTP
snippet: How MCP clients and servers exchange JSON-RPC messages: stdio for local processes, streamable HTTP (with optional SSE) for remote servers.

title: Building MCP servers in Python with FastMCP
snippet: The official Python SDK: declare tools with type hints and docstrings, and FastMCP generates schemas and handles the protocol lifecycle.

title: What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation?
snippet: RAG combines a retriever over an external corpus with a generator, grounding LLM answers in retrieved evidence to reduce hallucinations.

title: Chunking strategies for RAG pipelines
snippet: Fixed-size, recursive and semantic chunking compared: how chunk size and overlap affect recall and answer quality in retrieval pipelines.

title: Vector databases and pgvector for similarity search
snippet: Store embeddings in Postgres with pgvector: HNSW and IVFFlat indexes, cosine distance operators and trade-offs versus dedicated vector stores.

title: Text embeddings explained
snippet: Embeddings map text to dense vectors so that semantic similarity becomes geometric closeness; the backbone of retrieval and clustering.

title: LLM agents: planning, memory and tool use
snippet: An agent wraps an LLM in a loop of planning, tool calls and reflection; covers ReAct, task decomposition and memory architectures.

title: Designing tools that LLMs can actually use
snippet: Practical guidance on tool naming, argument schemas and docstrings: the model chooses tools based on descriptions, so write them for the model.

title: Function calling and structured outputs
snippet: How models emit JSON arguments for declared functions, and how strict schemas plus validation make tool invocation reliable.

title: Prompt engineering fundamentals
snippet: System prompts, few-shot examples, chain-of-thought and other patterns that reliably steer model behaviour across providers.

title: Evaluating LLM applications
snippet: From unit-style assertions to LLM-as-judge: building an evaluation loop that catches regressions in prompts, retrieval and tools.

title: Prompt injection and LLM security
snippet: Why prompt injection is the key attack class for tool-using LLMs, and which mitigations (least privilege, sandboxing, human review) actually help.

title: Fine-tuning versus RAG: when to use which
snippet: Comparing parameter updates against retrieval for injecting knowledge: cost, freshness and hallucination behaviour of each approach.
